articles.title: This cow uses tools like a primate—and scientists are stunned
articles.description: A cow named Veronika has stunned scientists by using tools in a flexible and purposeful way. She chooses different ends of a brush depending on the part of her body and adjusts her movements accordingly. This level of tool use is incredibly rare and was previously seen mainly in primates. articles.title: Scientists discover “overflow valve” in cells linked to Parkinson’s Disease
articles.description: Researchers have identified a crucial ion channel, TMEM175, that acts like an overflow valve in the cell’s recycling system. It regulates acidity inside lysosomes, ensuring they function properly. When TMEM175 is faulty, toxic buildup can occur, contributing to diseases like Parkinson’s. This breakt

articles.title: Scientists find immune cell linked to long COVID fatigue and symptoms
articles.description: Long COVID remains a frustrating medical mystery, affecting up to 1 in 10 people long after the initial infection fades. Now, scientists have uncovered a crucial clue hidden deep within the immune system. By analyzing individual immune cells, they identified a distinct molecular state in key white b

articles.title: Ukraine war briefing: Zelenskyy says US has linked security guarantees to ceding of Donbas
articles.description: Ukrainian president says peace deal proposed by US includes handing over land to Russia. What we know on day 1,492 Continue reading...

articles.title: Deepfake X-rays are so real even doctors can’t tell the difference
articles.description: Deepfake X-rays created by AI are now convincing enough to fool both doctors and AI models. In tests, radiologists had limited success identifying fake images, especially when they didn’t know they were being shown. This opens the door to risks like fraudulent medical claims and tampered diagnoses.

articles.title: Vivid dreams may be the secret to deeper, more restful sleep
articles.description: Vivid dreams might be doing more than just entertaining your mind at night. Researchers found that immersive dreaming can actually make sleep feel deeper and more refreshing, even when brain activity is high. articles.title: Scientists found a bug that generates its own heat in freezing cold
articles.description: Snow flies have an unexpected way of surviving freezing temperatures. They produce antifreeze proteins to block ice formation and can even generate their own heat. Scientists also found that their genes are unusually unique, and they feel less cold-related pain than other insects. These combined tra

articles.title: Scientists open a million-year-old time capsule beneath New Zealand
articles.description: Deep inside a cave, scientists uncovered fossils from 16 species, including a newfound kākāpō ancestor that may have been able to fly. articles.title: Silicon Valley Bank collapse renews calls to address disparities impacting entrepreneurs of color
articles.description: When customers at Silicon Valley Bank rushed to withdraw billions of dollars last month, venture capitalist Arlan Hamilton stepped in to help some of the founders of color who panicked about losing access to payroll funds.

articles.title: JAY GOLDBERG: Pressure on Carney to repeal the Online Streaming Act ramps up
articles.description: The Online Streaming Act would force streaming companies like Netflix, Amazon Prime and Disney Plus to provide 5% of their streaming revenue earned in Canada to the Canadian government... It also forces those streaming providers to follow Canadian content rules.

articles.title: Big-box stores could help slash emissions and save millions by putting solar panels on roofs. Why aren't more of them doing it?
articles.description: As the US attempts to wean itself off its heavy reliance on fossil fuels and shift to cleaner energy sources, many experts are eyeing a promising solution: your neighborhood big-box stores and shopping malls.

articles.title: Letters, March 23, 2026: ‘Canadians increasingly unhappy’
articles.description: Canada’s sad state Canada dropping to 25th place in the world happiness index is no surprise, given the state of the country.
